mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.91% as of July 2018 reflects recent Fed pause
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines do not exceed baseline returns by at least 50 basis points on average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising employment payroll levels signal strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which drives aggregate demand and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 149,128 thousand as of July 2018 shows sustained employment growth
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS momentum and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or employment growth periods produce negative average returns in more than 40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth accelerates consumer spending and signals robust aggregate demand, which increases corporate revenue expectations and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days when retail sales growth exceeds 0.5% month-over-month.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 507,533 million dollars as of July 2018
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when RSAFS growth exceeds 0.5% MoM, or Rank IC between RSAFS growth and forward returns is below 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects capacity utilization and manufacturing strength, signaling robust supply-side productivity. When INDPRO growth accelerates above its 12-month trend, it predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets reprrice growth expectations upward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeds 12-month average growth rate by 0.3 percentage points
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with accelerating productivity
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO acceleration and 5-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline combined with rising unemployment signals demand weakness and recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days. The mechanism activates when HOUST falls below its 12-month moving average while UNRATE rises simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 12-month moving average AND UNRATE rises 0.2 percentage points or more in prior month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,173 thousand units and UNRATE at 3.9% as of June 2018
regime_hint: late-cycle deceleration or early recession warning
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal produces Rank IC below 0.05 when tested independently against single-series alternatives over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration combined with elevated federal funds rate signals monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS is above 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 251.286 and FEDFUNDS at 1.91% as of July 2018
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation concerns
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (measured as joint signal Rank IC minus max single-series IC) is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate decline combined with accelerating wage growth (proxied by PAYEMS growth) signals tight labor market and wage-price spiral risk, which threatens corporate profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days in late-cycle regimes.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 4.0% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9% and PAYEMS at 149,128 thousand as of July 2018
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market and wage pressure
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal produces Rank IC below 0.04 when compared against single-series alternatives over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth deceleration signals weakening aggregate demand and earnings growth expectations. When quarterly GDPC1 growth falls below 2.0% annualized rate, S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over the subsequent 60 trading days as investors reprice growth and recession probability upward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate falls below 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18,507.2 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q1 2018
regime_hint: late-cycle deceleration or early recession regime
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when GDPC1 growth is below 2.0%, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and forward returns is below 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.
